experiment: Scoring the temporal-abstraction predictor: 18 k-sweep runs (k=2/8 vs k=4 anchor) and the Pick n-boost (n=8/arm) are trained with finals deliberately UNREAD; pre-registered predictions (disc_err_gap: k2<k4 on all tasks) are committed and public (blog Part 6). A CheetahRun jumpy-vs-vanilla gate (6 runs) is training as the cross-DOMAIN out-of-sample test, and a Cartesian run provides the out-of-task test. The scoring event fires once the last checkpoint dumps land (~1h): harvest everything, score the predictor, publish the update.
dev: The 5070 Ti dev box is grinding the final checkpoint mech-dumps (Cab k8 s2, then Cartesian + Cheetah signal dumps) under the shared-box memory cap (~5.6GB, Mahjong project untouched). Next dev items: seed-robustness dumps for the k4 reference signal, and the P2 exact-replay control that isolates reconstruction-error from open-loop chaos. After scoring: paper v2 (P1 outcome + P3 falsification into Sections 6-7) and the Part 6 blog update.
eta: Current batch: scoring event ~14:30 UTC today (last ckpt dumps finishing now); CheetahRun cross-domain gate (6 runs, started 10:40-12:40) trains until ~17:30-18:00 UTC; Part 6 update + Part 7 publish as each lands.

history.title: Part 1: From scratch to Phase 2 on HopperHop (world-model tutorial)
Part 2: The mechanism-check that saved a campaign (8 mirages)
Part 3: The latent was already the abstraction (value-sufficiency probe)
Part 4: The redundancy criterion — the paper (3/3 latent classes measured)
Part 5: Using abstraction right — aim at planning axes (iter-30 plan)
Part 6: Pre-registered predictions, published before results
Part 6 update: the scored predictor (ETA today ~14:30)
Part 7: Does the predictor transfer domains? (ETA today/tomorrow)
